commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Telegram `/login` command that runs OpenAI/Codex device-code auth through a reusable non-TTY login helper, updates OAuth failure copy, and exports the helper through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime`.

PR surface: Source +256, Tests +171. Total +427 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR, though source inspection shows a patch-introduced non-Telegram failure-copy regression.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR has a fixable P1 channel-copy bug, missing external real-behavior proof, and an auth/SDK boundary decision that automation should not settle.

target.title: Add Telegram /login Codex pairing flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a Telegram-native `/login` command for Codex/OpenAI device-code pairing.
- Reuse the existing OpenAI Codex `device-code` auth flow and profile persistence path through a reusable non-TTY login helper.
- Update hard OAuth refresh failure copy to recommend `/login codex` for Telegram users while preserving the terminal fallback.

## What Problem This Solves

Codex/OpenAI OAuth expiry currently leaves Telegram users with a terminal-only recovery path. On channel-only or remote gateways, that is a dead end for non-terminal operators. This adds an explicit owner-triggered `/login codex` path that sends the existing Codex device-code URL/code into the authorized Telegram chat or topic so the user can pair a fresh Codex login from a browser without exposing tokens or auto-starting auth in shared channels.

## Security / UX Notes

- `/login codex`, `/login openai`, and `/login openai-codex` normalize to provider `openai` and method `device-code`.
- Starting a Telegram login is owner-gated with `commands.ownerAllowFrom`; authorized non-owners are rejected.
- Active login flows are deduped per Telegram chat/topic/agent/provider.
- The Telegram wrapper sends only the provider’s device verification URL/code message and sanitized status text; it does not send or log OAuth tokens.
- The flow stays explicit and user-triggered rather than auto-starting OAuth after a model failure.

## Validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/check-extension-plugin-sdk-boundary.mjs --mode=plugin-sdk-internal`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files>`
- `git diff --check`

## Evidence

- Added tests for `/login codex` sending the device-code message before completion, owner-only rejection, and active-flow dedupe.
- Added OAuth classifier coverage for `token_invalidated`.
- Added failure-copy coverage showing hard OpenAI/Codex auth failures now recommend `/login codex`.
- Local focused validation passed for the exact changed auth, auto-reply, and Telegram command test files.

## Notes

- Extension-wide test typecheck still reports existing QA-lab crabline type errors unrelated to this PR; filtered output shows no Telegram `/login` type errors.

Refs #97984
